description: Scenic design, lighting, sound, rigging, costume. The disciplines that transform empty space into lived experience. Every sphere activation is theater without a script.

dome_relevance.11: Creativity — theatrical practice IS meaning-making in space
dome_relevance.12: Flourishing — the transformative experience of witnessing and participating

sphere_relevance.2: Infrastructure — rigging, power, structural support for activation
sphere_relevance.7: Activation — theatrical design IS activation design
sphere_relevance.8: Permanence — what remains after the performance

adjacent_disciplines.description: Light as material, circadian influence, mood, safety, wayfinding
Animating objects, environmental storytelling, non-verbal narrative

practitioners.name: Julie Taymor
practitioners.practice: Visual theater, puppet design, cross-cultural performance, spectacle as meaning
practitioners.approach: Theater's power is in making the invisible visible. The Lion King's actors carry their puppets openly — the double event of actor and character creates the space for imagination. The audience does the work. The design creates the conditions for awe.
practitioners.body_of_work: The Lion King (Broadway) — most commercially successful production in theater history. Titus (film), The Magic Flute (Met Opera), Spider-Man: Turn Off the Dark. Blends Indonesian shadow puppetry, Japanese Bunraku, and African ritual with Western theater.
practitioners.why_instructive: She designs Keltner awe triggers deliberately — vastness (spectacle), accommodation (double event), collective effervescence (shared wonder). Her Lion King IS a sphere activation that has run nightly for 27 years.

dome_relevance.11: Creativity — cross-cultural synthesis, making invisible visible

sphere_relevance.7: Activation — the Lion King as 27-year ongoing activation, awe by design
sphere_relevance.8: Permanence — cultural permanence through theatrical innovation
sphere_relevance.10: Catalyst — her approach has influenced every major theater production since

practitioners.name: Prospero
practitioners.creator: William Shakespeare
practitioners.practice: Space transformation, theatrical magic, using an island as a stage
practitioners.approach: The island IS the theater. Every natural feature — the cave, the shore, the grove — becomes a stage for transformation. Prospero's magic is really theatrical direction: he orchestrates encounters, creates spectacles, designs emotional arcs. Then he breaks his staff — the designer lets the space be itself again.
practitioners.body_of_work: Transforms a wild island into a designed experience through which his enemies journey toward understanding. Creates the masque (layer 7 activation), orchestrates encounters (layer 5 social), and ultimately releases control (layer 10 catalyst — the space activates without him).
practitioners.why_instructive: He's the sphere producer — someone who transforms a place through designed experience. His breaking of the staff at the end is the crucial insight: the activation must eventually sustain itself without the designer.

dome_relevance.11: Creativity — theatrical magic as the ultimate creative practice

sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — using a place's history (exile, isolation) as material
sphere_relevance.7: Activation — the island experience IS the activation
sphere_relevance.8: Permanence — what remains when the designer withdraws
sphere_relevance.10: Catalyst — breaking the staff. The space continues without the artist.

practitioners.name: Theaster Gates
practitioners.practice: Art as urban infrastructure, abandoned building activation, Black material culture
practitioners.approach: Buy abandoned buildings on the South Side of Chicago for $1. Transform them into libraries, galleries, listening rooms, archives. Art practice IS real estate practice IS community development. The building is the medium. The neighborhood is the canvas. Culture is infrastructure.
practitioners.body_of_work: Dorchester Projects — abandoned houses became Archive House, Listening House, Black Cinema House. Stony Island Arts Bank — abandoned bank became cultural center. Rebuild Foundation. His work has increased property values and community engagement across Grand Crossing and Washington Park.
practitioners.why_instructive: He IS a sphere producer in practice. Every project activates a physical space (sphere layer 7), creates permanence (layer 8), changes policy perception (layer 9), and catalyzes adjacent activation (layer 10). He proves the sphere schema works.

dome_relevance.5: Housing — abandoned buildings as housing for culture
dome_relevance.8: Community — art practice as community building
dome_relevance.11: Creativity — the building itself as artistic medium

sphere_relevance.1: Parcel — buying abandoned parcels, changing ownership and use
sphere_relevance.4: Economic — $1 purchases becoming multi-million dollar cultural assets
sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — honoring building history while creating new use
sphere_relevance.7: Activation — every building becomes an activated cultural space
sphere_relevance.8: Permanence — institutions that outlast the initial intervention
sphere_relevance.9: Policy — changed how Chicago thinks about vacant property
sphere_relevance.10: Catalyst — each project catalyzes the next block

practitioners.name: Robert Wilson
practitioners.practice: Visual theater, time-based installation, light as primary material
practitioners.approach: Time is material. Light is material. Movement at glacial speed creates a different kind of attention. Einstein on the Beach ran 5 hours with no intermission. His theater doesn't tell stories — it creates states of consciousness through duration, light, and geometry.
practitioners.body_of_work: Einstein on the Beach (with Philip Glass), the CIVIL warS, Monsters of Grace. Watermill Center — his laboratory on Long Island. 50+ years of theater that treats time and light as primary materials.
practitioners.why_instructive: His work demonstrates what Keltner calls 'vastness' and 'accommodation' through duration. Sphere activations that run for hours create different neurological states than hour-long events. He proves that slow activation is powerful activation.

dome_relevance.11: Creativity — time itself as creative medium

sphere_relevance.6: Temporal — duration as design material, time perception altered by environment
sphere_relevance.7: Activation — slow activation, extended duration, altered consciousness
